channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Trump team urges Oregon judge to end restraining order blocking National Guard
item.description: Judge Karin Immergut said she will decide by Monday whether to dissolve a restraining order blocking President Donald Trump from deploying National Guard troops in Portland.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Poway Councilmember Tony Blain charged with four felonies days before recall election
item.description: The San Diego County District Attorney’s office has filed five charges, including four felonies, against embattled Poway City Councilmember Tony Blain.
item.dc:creator: Jennifer Franco, Fox 5

item.title: Over 1,000 high school students across San Diego County walk out for environment
item.description: More than 1,000 students across 19 high schools in San Diego County participated in a state-wide walkout on Friday, urging lawmakers to pass climate related legislation.
item.dc:creator: Chris Ponce, Fox 5

item.title: Trump heads to 3-country sprint in Asia, meeting with Xi as government shutdown drags on at home
item.description: ABOARD AIR FORCE ONE (AP) — President Donald Trump headed for Asia Friday night for the first time this term, a trip where he’s expected to work on investment deals and peace efforts before meeting face-to-face with Chinese President Xi Jinping to try to deescalate a trade war.
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE L. PRICE and MARK SCHIEFELBEIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Dallas pediatrician sued over providing hormone treatments to teens surrenders medical license
item.description: After Texas Attorney General Ken Paxton sued last year, Dr. May Lau said she is moving to Oregon and doesn't need a Texas license anymore.
item.dc:creator: Texas Tribune, By Xiomara Moore, KPRC 2

item.title: DeSantis likely to sign bill allowing Ivermectin over the counter
item.description: In a social media post, Gov. Ron DeSantis said he’s pushing the legislature to pass a bill involving Ivermectin and he plans to sign it.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Protesters gather as Homeland Security Secretary Noem visits Minnesota for ICE briefing
item.description: Homeland Security Secretary Kristi Noem visited Minnesota Friday to discuss federal immigration enforcement efforts in Minnesota. Protests took place outside the event.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Thamer, Jon Collins, and Matt Sepic, MPR News

item.title: Congresswoman scammed in her home by fake cleaning crew, police say
item.description: Del. Eleanor Holmes Norton (D-D.C.) was scammed on Thursday by a group claiming to be a cleaning crew, according to authorities.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Eleanor Holmes Norton, an 88-year-old Dem congressional delegate, scammed by phony 'cleaning crew' at DC home
item.description: A Washington, D.C., congressional delegate was targeted in a brazen credit card fraud scheme after suspects posed as maintenance workers to access her home and finances.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump administration won't tap contingency fund to keep food aid flowing, memo says
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Trump administration is rejecting the idea of using roughly $5 billion in contingency funds to keep food aid flowing into November amid the government shutdown, according to a Department of Agriculture memo that surfaced Friday.
item.dc:creator: KEVIN FREKING, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Reagan Foundation becomes the latest US institution drawn into Donald Trump's controversies
item.description: The Ronald Reagan Presidential Foundation and Institute is drawing blowback and praise for arguing that a Canadian government advertisement playing in U.S. markets misrepresented the 40th president’s words to blast President Donald Trump’s tariff policies.
item.dc:creator: Bill Barrow And Thalia Beaty, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump to jet off to Asia as North Korea fires ballistic missiles and China trade questions loom
item.description: Trump's Asia summit tour includes meetings with Chinese President Xi Jinping and other regional leaders as trade tensions with Beijing escalate.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Two candidates vie for DFL endorsement in race to become next Minnesota U.S. senator
item.description: Lt. Gov. Peggy Flanagan and U.S. Rep. Angie Craig joined Cathy Wurzer on Morning Edition to talk about their ambitions to win the DFL Party endorsement in Minnesota’s 2026 U.S. Senate race.
item.dc:creator: Cathy Wurzer and Matthew Alvarez, MPR News

item.title: New Jersey early voting 2025: When locations open and how to find them
item.description: NEW JERSEY (PIX11) -- While Election Day is fast approaching, New Jersey residents will be able to cast their ballot as early as this weekend. In 2021, New Jersey established in-person early voting for registered voters at General Election polling locations.
item.dc:creator: Ben Mitchell, PIX 11

item.title: Transportation Secretary Duffy ‘can’t guarantee’ on-time flights during shutdown
item.description: Transportation Secretary Sean Duffy warned Thursday of possible flight delays and cancellations during the government shutdown, saying the federal safety system depends on air traffic controllers working without pay. “I can’t guarantee you that your flight’s going to be on time.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: NYC Mayor Eric Adams endorses Andrew Cuomo
item.description: NEW YORK CITY (PIX11) — New York City Mayor Eric Adams endorsed independent candidate former New York Gov. Andrew Cuomo as his successor on Thursday. During Wednesday night’s final mayoral debate, Cuomo was the only candidate who said he’d accept an endorsement from Adams when asked by moderators.
item.dc:creator: Video credit: Henry Rosoff, NewsNation

item.title: Stephen A. Smith apologizes to Crockett over 'street verbiage' remark
item.description: Pundit Stephen A. Smith apologized this week for comments he made criticizing Rep. Jasmine Crockett (D-Texas) and her approach to politics.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, NewsNation

item.title: Why your mail may not get postmarked the day it's sent
item.description: Close to a deadline for mailing your ballot, tax return or legal document? To make sure it gets a postmark on time under the latest USPS changes, you may want to send it early or visit a post office.
item.dc:creator: Hansi Lo Wang, MPR News

item.title: Senate's votes on pay for federal workers fail as shutdown continues
item.description: Air traffic controllers and military service members are among those who are working without pay during the shutdown.
item.dc:creator: Jackie Koppell, NewsNation

item.title: 5 takeaways from the final New York City mayor's debate
item.description: There was no shortage of fireworks between Democrat Zohran Mamdani, independent Andrew Cuomo and Republican Curtis Sliwa.
item.dc:creator: Julia Manchester, NewsNation

item.title: California leaders write letter to Hegseth, demand answers for artillery fire over I-5
item.description: CAMP PENDLETON, Calif. (FOX 5/KUSI) -- California Democrats sent a letter to Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth on Wednesday regarding shrapnel that struck California Highway Patrol (CHP) vehicles during Vice President JD Vance’s visit to Camp Pendleton.
item.dc:creator: Chris Ponce, Fox 5

item.title: Wyoming Capitol evacuated after report of suspected explosive device
item.description: The Wyoming Capitol in Cheyenne was evacuated on Tuesday after a report about a suspected explosive device, according to authorities.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, NewsNation

item.title: Antifa infiltrating Seattle homeless services, report finds
item.description: NewsNation found evidence of activists confronting volunteers and disrupting encampment cleanup efforts.
item.dc:creator: Rich McHugh, NewsNation

item.title: Preservation group urges pause in White House East Wing demolition
item.description: The destruction of a portion of the White House’s East Wing to create the ballroom has hit a nerve among critics.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, NewsNation

item.title: Minnesota cities can't regulate guns. St. Paul is introducing an assault weapon ban anyway
item.description: St. Paul city leaders are expected introduce a gun ordinance Wednesday, as part of an ongoing push from several Minnesota cities to get the state to repeal a law that prevents stricter municipal gun laws.
item.dc:creator: MPR News Staff, MPR News

item.title: Democrats block government funding bill for 12th time after marathon floor protest
item.description: Senate Democrats voted Wednesday for a 12th time to block a House-passed bill to reopen the government and keep it funded through Nov. 21. Democrats blocked the funding measure — a clean continuing resolution that would prolong Biden-era funding levels — after liberal Sen. Jeff Merkley (D-Ore.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, NewsNation

item.title: DeSantis: Progressive to issue nearly $1 billion in credits to Florida policyholders
item.description: “Thanks to Commissioner (Michael) Yaworsky, he has secured almost a billion dollars in credits for Progressive auto insurance policyholders,” DeSantis said.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Judge agrees to extend block on Guard deployment in Chicago while awaiting Supreme Court ruling
item.description: As the U.S. Supreme Court weighs whether to clear the way for the National Guard in Chicago, a federal judge on Wednesday agreed to extend a two-week block on Guard deployment in the Chicago area by 30 days.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Trump pick to lead watchdog agency withdraws after offensive text messages revealed
item.description: President Trump’s pick to lead a federal watchdog agency withdrew from consideration Tuesday evening after his offensive text messages were made public and GOP senators revolted.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Government shutdown expected to delay SNAP, CalFresh benefits
item.description: As the government marks 21 days into its shutdown, officials are stating that CalFresh and Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program (SNAP) benefits may be delayed for millions of Californians.
item.dc:creator: Chris Ponce, Fox 5

item.title: Florida sets record for python removal with help of new partner
item.description: Governor Ron Desantis shared the spotlight Tuesday with an 11-foot Burmese python, making it clear the fight to protect the Everglades from invasive species only continues.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Senate Republicans go to White House in show of unity
item.description: As the government shutdown enters its fourth week, Senate Republicans are headed to the White House — not for urgent talks on how to end it, but for a display of unity with President Donald Trump as they refuse to negotiate on any Democratic demands.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: White House starts demolishing part of the East Wing
item.description: Dramatic photos show construction equipment tearing into the East Wing façade and windows, though the federal agency that oversees such projects has not approved President Trump's 90,000-square-foot, $250 million ballroom.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Florida lawmakers focus on affordability ahead of 2026 session
item.description: Last year was supposed to be the year of affordability, but the same concerns still linger as lawmakers head into the 2026 session.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: As deadline for Trump’s colleges compact looms, schools signal dissent
item.description: Of the original nine schools that received the Trump administration's Compact for Academic Excellence in Higher Education, the majority have indicated they are not planning on signing.
item.dc:creator: Elissa Nadworny, MPR News

item.title: Trump announces end to U.S. aid to Colombia
item.description: President Donald Trump said Sunday he would slash U.S. funding to Colombia because the country’s leader “does nothing to stop” drug production.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: As they go unpaid, Capitol Police are a daily reminder for lawmakers of shutdown pain
item.description: The more than 2,000 officers on the force missed their first full paycheck Oct. 10, leaving them to go without pay at a moment when the officials they protect face growing threats and violence.
item.dc:creator: Sam Gringlas, MPR News
